subject: architecture modules
new: based on LLVM 16 (ARM, PPC) or LLVM 18 (others)
truth: Architecture modules are now based on LLVM 16 or LLVM 18 instead of LLVM 7, causing breaking changes in disassembly output and APIs.
quote: While in `v5` the most up-to-date module was based on `LLVM 7`, the refactored modules are based on `LLVM 16` (`ARM`, `PPC`) and `LLVM 18` (the others)!

from_fact: based on llvm 16 (arm, ppc) or llvm 18 (others)

subject: architecture modules
new: closer to original LLVM code (Auto-Sync enabled modules)
truth: Architecture modules now follow LLVM code structure more closely, breaking existing code that relied on Capstone-specific APIs or data structures.
quote: Because most of the update steps are automated now, the architecture modules must fit this update mechanism. Which means they move closer to the original LLVM code. On the flip site it brings many breaking changes.

from_fact: closer to original llvm code (auto-sync enabled modules)

subject: cs_insn.illegal flag
new: cs_insn.illegal flag added; if set, instruction is decoded correctly but considered illegal
truth: Stop assuming all decoded instructions are legal; check cs_insn.illegal flag to detect instructions with invalid operands or illegal context.
quote: The cs_insn.illegal flag was added. If it is set the instruction is decoded correctly but is considered illegal. This happens for instructions which use invalid operands or are in an illegal context.

from_fact: cs_insn.illegal flag added; if set, instruction is decoded correctly but considered illegal

subject: AArch64 EXACTFPIMM operand
old: EXACTFPIMM operand did not set the fp field
new: EXACTFPIMM operand now also sets the fp field
truth: Stop relying on EXACTFPIMM operand not setting the fp field; it now sets the fp field as well.
quote: The EXACTFPIMM operand also sets the fp field.

from_fact: exactfpimm operand did not set the fp field
exactfpimm operand now also sets the fp field

subject: MIPS mode for 64-bit pointer decoding
new: CS_MODE_MIPS_PTR64 is now required to decode 64-bit pointers
truth: You must add CS_MODE_MIPS_PTR64 to the mode when opening a MIPS engine to decode 64-bit pointers like jumps and calls.
quote: CS_MODE_MIPS_PTR64 is now required to decode 64-bit pointers, like jumps and calls (for example: `jal $t0`).

from_fact: cs_mode_mips_ptr64 is now required to decode 64-bit pointers

subject: default alias printing behaviour
old: Capstone previously did not print instruction aliases by default (or had a different default)
new: Capstone now prints instruction aliases by default
truth: Capstone now prints instruction aliases by default; you must set CS_OPT_SYNTAX_NO_ALIAS_TEXT to suppress them.
quote: The default behaviour of Capstone is to print those aliases whenever applicable

from_fact: capstone previously did not print instruction aliases by default (or had a different default)
capstone now prints instruction aliases by default

subject: eBPF legacy packet instruction syntax
old: GNU-style mnemonics were used for legacy packet instructions.
new: LLVM-style mnemonics are now used (e.g. `ldabsw [skb-0x8]`).
truth: eBPF legacy packet instructions now use LLVM-style mnemonics instead of GNU-style (e.g. `ldabsw [skb-0x8]`).
quote: Updated the syntax for eBPF legacy packet instructions (similar to LLVM mnemonics, not GNU ones (E.g. `ldabsw [skb-0x8]`). `skb` is the socket buffer.

from_fact: gnu-style mnemonics were used for legacy packet instructions.

subject: eBPF ATOMIC class instruction mnemonics
new: Linux-style mnemonics are now used (e.g. `acmpxchg64`).
truth: eBPF ATOMIC class instructions now use Linux mnemonics instead of GNU ones (e.g. `acmpxchg64` instead of `axchg`).
quote: Added support for eBPF `ATOMIC` class instructions (using Linux mnemonics, not GNU ones. E.g. `acmpxchg64` instead of `axchg`)

subject: eBPF immediate and offset signedness interpretation
old: Immediate and offset operands were interpreted with a different signedness.
new: The signedness interpretation of immediate and offset operands has been corrected.
truth: The signedness interpretation of eBPF immediate and offset operands has been corrected, which may change how existing disassembly output is displayed.
quote: Corrected the signedness interpretation of `immidiate` and `offset` operands

from_fact: immediate and offset operands were interpreted with a different signedness.
the signedness interpretation of immediate and offset operands has been corrected.

subject: cs_insn structure
old: cs_insn had no alias_id field; alias instructions were not distinguished from real instructions
new: cs_insn now has alias_id field; cs_insn->is_alias flag is set for aliases; cs_insn->alias_id holds the alias id
truth: cs_insn now has alias_id and is_alias fields; alias instructions are separated from real instructions and you must check is_alias and use alias_id instead of assuming id is the alias
quote: Instruction alias are now properly separated from real instructions. The cs_insn->is_alias flag is set, if the decoded instruction is an alias. The real instruction id is still set in cs_insn->id. The alias id is set in cs_insn->alias_id.

from_fact: cs_insn had no alias_id field; alias instructions were not distinguished from real instructions
cs_insn now has alias_id field; cs_insn->is_alias flag is set for aliases; cs_insn->alias_id holds the alias id

subject: cs_option detail mode for aliases
old: cs_option with CS_OPT_DETAIL used CS_OPT_ON/CS_OPT_OFF only; alias instructions always returned alias operands
new: cs_option(handle, CS_OPT_DETAIL, CS_OPT_DETAIL_REAL | CS_OPT_ON) now available to force real instruction operands for aliases; default behavior returns alias operands
truth: cs_option with CS_OPT_DETAIL now accepts CS_OPT_DETAIL_REAL | CS_OPT_ON to get real instruction operands for aliases; default returns alias operands
quote: If you always want the real instruction detail decoded (also for alias instructions), you can enable the option with cs_option(handle, CS_OPT_DETAIL, CS_OPT_DETAIL_REAL | CS_OPT_ON).

from_fact: cs_option with cs_opt_detail used cs_opt_on/cs_opt_off only; alias instructions always returned alias operands
cs_option(handle, cs_opt_detail, cs_opt_detail_real | cs_opt_on) now available to force real instruction operands for aliases; default behavior returns alias operands

subject: cstool alias handling
old: cstool had no -r flag; alias instructions always showed alias operands
new: cstool now supports -r flag to show real instruction operands for aliases; without -r shows alias operands
truth: cstool now has -r flag to show real instruction operands for aliases; default behavior shows alias operands
quote: For the cstool you can enable it with the -r flag. Without -r you get the alias operand set, if the instruction is an alias.

from_fact: cstool had no -r flag; alias instructions always showed alias operands
cstool now supports -r flag to show real instruction operands for aliases; without -r shows alias operands

subject: csh and cs_option value parameter type
old: csh and cs_option's value argument was not uintptr_t
new: csh and cs_option's value argument is now uintptr_t
truth: csh and cs_option's value argument is now of type uintptr_t instead of its previous type
quote: csh and cs_option's argument value are now of type uintptr_t to ensure they can be cast between integer and pointer without issues.

from_fact: csh and cs_option's value argument was not uintptr_t
csh and cs_option's value argument is now uintptr_t

subject: cstool architecture option syntax
old: cstool used separate mechanism for architecture-specific options
new: cstool now uses cstool <arch>+<option> syntax to enable architecture-specific options
truth: cstool now requires architecture-specific options to be enabled with cstool <arch>+<option> syntax
quote: cstool: Architecture specific options can now be enabled with cstool <arch>+<option>.

from_fact: cstool used separate mechanism for architecture-specific options
cstool now uses cstool <arch>+<option> syntax to enable architecture-specific options

subject: make build system
truth: Stop using `make` to build Capstone; it is deprecated and no longer supported.
quote: Building Capstone with `make` is deprecated now and is no longer supported. Build files will be removed in the next release.

subject: Java bindings
truth: Stop using Java bindings for Capstone; they are abandoned and not up-to-date in the Alpha release.
quote: The Java and Ocaml bindings were abandoned for a while now. So in the Alpha release they are not yet up-to-date.

subject: Ocaml bindings
truth: Stop using Ocaml bindings for Capstone; they are abandoned and not up-to-date in the Alpha release.
quote: The Java and Ocaml bindings were abandoned for a while now. So in the Alpha release they are not yet up-to-date.

subject: Python 2 and Python <3.8 support
truth: Stop using Python 2 or Python versions below 3.8 with Capstone; they are dropped in the v5 and next branch.
quote: Python 2 and <3.8 are dropped in the `v5` and `next` branch.

subject: Post-index memory access operand representation
old: disponent stored as separate reg/imm operand
new: disponent set in MEMORY operand
truth: Post-index memory access no longer has a separate reg/imm operand for the disponent; the disponent is now set in the MEMORY operand.
quote: Post-index memory access has the disponent now set in the `MEMORY` operand! No longer as separated `reg`/`imm` operand.

from_fact: disponent stored as separate reg/imm operand

subject: mem.disp sign handling
new: mem.disp is always positive, subtracted flag indicates subtraction
truth: mem.disp is now always positive; use the subtracted flag to determine if the value should be subtracted.
quote: `mem.disp` is now always positive and the `subtracted` flag indicates if it should be subtracted.

from_fact: mem.disp is always positive, subtracted flag indicates subtraction

subject: ARM_CC enum
new: ARMCC
truth: Replace ARM_CC with ARMCC; the enum values have also changed.
quote: `ARM_CC` → `ARMCC` and value change

subject: ARMCC_EQ value
new: ARMCC_EQ == 0, ARMCC_INVALID != 0
truth: ARMCC_EQ is now 0 and ARMCC_INVALID is non-zero.
quote: `ARMCC_EQ == 0` but `ARMCC_INVALID != 0`

subject: System registers storage
new: system registers separated with more detail
truth: System registers are no longer stored in cs_arm->reg; they are now separated into their own field with more detail.
quote: System registers are no longer saved in `cs_arm->reg`, but are separated and have more detail.

from_fact: system registers separated with more detail

subject: System operand encoding
old: System operands used old Capstone encoding
new: System operands now use LLVM's SYSm value encoding
truth: System operands now use the LLVM SYSm value encoding instead of the old Capstone encoding.
quote: System operands have now the encoding of LLVM (SYSm value mostly)

from_fact: system operands used old capstone encoding
system operands now use llvm's sysm value encoding

subject: Alias instructions from instruction enum
old: alias instructions present in instruction enum
truth: Multiple alias instructions have been removed from the instruction enum; aliases are now disassembled as their real instructions with an additional alias identifier.
quote: Multiple instructions which were only alias were removed from the instruction enum.

from_fact: alias instructions present in instruction enum

subject: Instruction groups naming
new: renamed to CPU features
truth: Instruction groups that were actually CPU features have been renamed to reflect that they are CPU features.
quote: Instruction groups, which actually were CPU features, were renamed to reflect that.

subject: CPU features checking strictness
new: stricter checking (MCLASS, V8 etc.)
truth: CPU features are now checked more strictly (MCLASS, V8, etc.), which may cause different instruction decoding depending on enabled features.
quote: CPU features get checked more strictly (`MCLASS`, `V8` etc.)

subject: writeback member location
new: writeback member moved to detail
truth: Stop accessing writeback at the top level; access it from the detail struct instead.
quote: `writeback` member was moved to detail.

subject: Register alias printing default
old: register aliases (r15=pc etc.) printed by default
new: not printed by default; opt-in via CS_OPT_SYNTAX_CS_REG_ALIAS
truth: Register aliases (r15=pc etc.) are no longer printed by default; enable CS_OPT_SYNTAX_CS_REG_ALIAS to restore them (with significant runtime cost).
quote: Register alias (`r15 = pc` etc.) are not printed if LLVM doesn't do it. Old Capstone register alias can be enabled by `CS_OPT_SYNTAX_CS_REG_ALIAS`.

from_fact: register aliases (r15=pc etc.) printed by default
not printed by default; opt-in via cs_opt_syntax_cs_reg_alias

subject: arm_op.imm type
old: arm_op.imm was a smaller integer type
new: arm_op.imm is now int64_t
truth: arm_op.imm type changed to int64_t, which may cause truncation or type mismatch in existing code.
quote: Immediate values (`arm_op.imm`) type changed to `int64_t`

from_fact: arm_op.imm was a smaller integer type

subject: mem.lshift field
truth: The mem.lshift field has been removed; it duplicated information already in shift.
quote: The `mem.lshift` field was removed. It was not set properly before and just duplicates information in `shift`

subject: Operand access type field type
new: enum cs_ac_type access
truth: The operand access field type changed from uint8_t to enum cs_ac_type.
quote: | Operand access type | Previously, operand access type is stored in the `uint8_t access;` field within operand details.

subject: ARMCC_EQ value
new: ARMCC_EQ == 0
truth: ARMCC_EQ is now 0; ARMCC_INVALID is now non-zero, reversing their previous relationship.
quote: `ARMCC_EQ == 0` but `ARMCC_INVALID != 0`

subject: Post-index memory access operand
old: post-index memory access had a separate reg/imm operand
new: post-index memory access has the displacement set inside the MEMORY operand
truth: Stop reading post-index displacement from a separate reg/imm operand; read it from the MEMORY operand instead.
quote: Post-index memory access has the disponent now set int the `MEMORY` operand! No longer as separated `reg`/`imm` operand.

from_fact: post-index memory access had a separate reg/imm operand
post-index memory access has the displacement set inside the memory operand

subject: System registers location
old: System registers were stored in cs_arm->reg
new: System registers are separated out with more detail
truth: System registers are no longer in cs_arm->reg; they are stored separately with more detail.
quote: System registers are no longer saved in `cs_arm->reg`, but are separated and have more detail.

from_fact: system registers were stored in cs_arm->reg
system registers are separated out with more detail

subject: CPU feature checking strictness
old: CPU features like MCLASS, V8 were checked loosely
new: CPU features like MCLASS, V8 are checked more strictly
truth: CPU features (MCLASS, V8, etc.) are now checked more strictly, which may cause previously-decoded instructions to fail or decode differently.
quote: CPU features get checked more strictly (`MCLASS`, `V8` etc.)

from_fact: cpu features like mclass, v8 were checked loosely
cpu features like mclass, v8 are checked more strictly

subject: writeback member location
new: writeback was moved into the detail struct
truth: The writeback member has been moved into the detail struct instead of being a top-level member.
quote: `writeback` member was moved to detail.

from_fact: writeback was moved into the detail struct

subject: architecture name
new: AArch64
truth: Stop using 'ARM64' as the architecture name; use 'AArch64' instead.
quote: AArch64 (formerly ARM64)

subject: arm64_vas enum
new: AArch64Layout_VectorLayout
truth: Stop using the enum name 'arm64_vas'; use 'AArch64Layout_VectorLayout' instead.
quote: `arm64_vas` renamed to `AArch64Layout_VectorLayout`

subject: Register alias printing (x29 = fp etc.)
old: register aliases were printed by default
new: register aliases are not printed by default; enable with CS_OPT_SYNTAX_CS_REG_ALIAS
truth: Stop relying on register aliases like 'fp' for x29 being printed by default; enable CS_OPT_SYNTAX_CS_REG_ALIAS if needed, but beware of runtime cost.
quote: Register alias (`x29 = fp` etc.) are not printed if LLVM doesn't do it. Old Capstone register alias can be enabled by `CS_OPT_SYNTAX_CS_REG_ALIAS`. WARNING: This option uses a naive search and replace strategy to patch the register names in the asm text. And hence adds significant runtime at scale, if enabled.

from_fact: register aliases were printed by default
register aliases are not printed by default; enable with cs_opt_syntax_cs_reg_alias

subject: AArch64CC_* condition code enum values
new: AArch64CC_EQ == 0, AArch64CC_INVALID != 0
truth: Stop assuming AArch64CC_INVALID is 0 or that AArch64CC_EQ is non-zero; AArch64CC_EQ is now 0 and AArch64CC_INVALID is non-zero.
quote: `AArch64CC_EQ == 0` but `AArch64CC_INVALID != 0`

subject: PPC_BC branch conditions
new: completely rewritten branch conditions saving all detail known about the bits
truth: Stop relying on the old PPC_BC branch condition bit layout; the conditions were completely rewritten and now save all detail known about the bits.
quote: The branch conditions were completely rewritten and save now all detail known about the bits.

from_fact: completely rewritten branch conditions saving all detail known about the bits

subject: PPC predicate enums
new: renamed predicate enums
truth: Stop using the old predicate enum names; they were renamed due to the branch condition changes.
quote: Predicate enums were renamed due to the changes to the branch conditions.

subject: PPC instruction aliases (e.g. BF)
old: instruction aliases like BF in the instruction enum
truth: Stop using instruction aliases like BF from the instruction enum; they were removed and alias information is now provided separately in their own fields.
quote: Many instruction alias (e.g. BF) were removed from the instruction enum (see new alias feature below).

from_fact: instruction aliases like bf in the instruction enum

subject: ppc_ops_crx
truth: Stop using ppc_ops_crx; it was removed.
quote: ppc_ops_crx was removed.

subject: PPC (RA|0) zero case register
old: register 0 used literally for (RA|0) zero case
new: PPC_REG_ZERO register (name '0') used for (RA|0) zero case
truth: Stop using register 0 directly for the (RA|0) zero case; the PPC_REG_ZERO register (with name '0') is now used instead.
quote: The (RA|0) cases (see ISA for details) for which 0 is used, the PPC_REG_ZERO register is used. The register name of it is 0.

from_fact: register 0 used literally for (ra|0) zero case
ppc_reg_zero register (name '0') used for (ra|0) zero case

subject: PPC condition register 'so' bit name
new: 'un' bit name
truth: Stop using the 'so' bit name for the PPC condition register; it was renamed to 'un'.
quote: The verbose condition register names changes the so bit name to un. Just as LLVM does.

subject: SYSZ
new: SystemZ
truth: Stop using SYSZ everywhere; use SystemZ instead.
quote: `SYSZ` was everywhere renamed to `SystemZ` to match the LLVM naming.

subject: m68k_op_mem.in_disp and m68k_op_mem.out_disp
new: signed fields
truth: m68k_op_mem.in_disp and m68k_op_mem.out_disp are now signed instead of unsigned; code that treats them as unsigned will get wrong values for negative displacements.
quote: These fields are now signed instead of unsigned.

subject: M68K_OP_MEM storage layout
old: memory operands stored base register in op->reg and absolute address in op->imm
new: memory operands store base register in m68k_op_mem.base_reg and absolute address in m68k_op_mem.address
truth: Memory operands now store base registers in m68k_op_mem.base_reg and absolute addresses in m68k_op_mem.address; op->reg and op->imm are no longer used for memory operands.
quote: Memory operands now store base registers in `m68k_op_mem.base_reg` and absolute addresses in `m68k_op_mem.address`; `op->reg` and `op->imm` are only used for register and immediate operands.

from_fact: memory operands stored base register in op->reg and absolute address in op->imm
memory operands store base register in m68k_op_mem.base_reg and absolute address in m68k_op_mem.address

subject: Python module capstone.arm64
new: capstone.aarch64 module
truth: The Python binding module capstone.arm64 has been moved to capstone.aarch64, with all ARM64_* constants renamed to AARCH64_*.
quote: the `capstone.arm64` module was moved to `capstone.aarch64`

subject: Python module capstone.sysz_const
new: capstone.systemz_const module
truth: The Python binding module capstone.sysz_const has been moved to capstone.systemz_const, with all SYSZ_* constants renamed to SYSTEMZ_*.
quote: the `capstone.sysz_const` module was moved to `capstone.systemz_const`

subject: ARM_CC / arm_cc naming
new: ARMCC and ARMCC_CondCodes
truth: ARM_CC has been renamed to ARMCC and arm_cc has been renamed to ARMCC_CondCodes.
quote: the `CAPSTONE_ARM_COMPAT_HEADER` will only define macros for the `ARM_CC -> ARMCC` and `arm_cc -> ARMCC_CondCodes` renaming

subject: compatibility layer for type identifiers and detail names
old: compatibility layer for type identifiers and detail names
truth: Stop relying on any compatibility layer for type identifiers and detail names; there is no compatibility layer at all.
quote: There is no compatibility layer for type identifiers and detail names at the moment.

from_fact: compatibility layer for type identifiers and detail names

subject: SYSTEMZ_CC_O and SYSTEMZ_CC_INVALID values
new: SYSTEMZ_CC_O = 0 and SYSTEMZ_CC_INVALID != 0 (values swapped to match LLVM)
truth: SYSTEMZ_CC_O is now 0 and SYSTEMZ_CC_INVALID is now non-zero (matching LLVM values); code that relied on the old enum ordering will break.
quote: `SYSTEMZ_CC_O = 0` and `SYSTEMZ_CC_INVALID != 0`

from_fact: systemz_cc_o = 0 and systemz_cc_invalid != 0 (values swapped to match llvm)

subject: ARM64 enum/type names
old: ARM64_* (e.g. CS_ARCH_ARM64, ARM64_INS_*, ARM64_REG_*, ARM64_OP_*, ARM64_EXT_*, ARM64_SFT_*, ARM64_CC_*) and arm64_* types (arm64_reg, arm64_cc, cs_arm64, arm64_extender, arm64_shifter, arm64_vas) and detail->arm64
new: AArch64_* / aarch64_* / detail->aarch64
truth: All ARM64_* enum prefixes, arm64_* type names, and detail->arm64 have been renamed to AArch64_*, aarch64_*, and detail->aarch64 respectively.
quote: Simple renaming from `ARM64` to `AArch64`

from_fact: arm64_* (e.g. cs_arch_arm64, arm64_ins_*, arm64_reg_*, arm64_op_*, arm64_ext_*, arm64_sft_*, arm64_cc_*) and arm64_* types (arm64_reg, arm64_cc, cs_arm64, arm64_extender, arm64_shifter, arm64_vas) and detail->arm64

subject: SYSZ enum/type names
old: SYSZ_* (e.g. CS_ARCH_SYSZ, SYSZ_INS_*, SYSZ_REG_*, SYSZ_OP_*, SYSZ_CC_*) and sysz_* types (sysz_reg, sysz_cc, cs_sysz, sysz_op_type, sysz_op_mem, sysz_op) and detail->sysz
new: SYSTEMZ_* / systemz_* / detail->systemz
truth: All SYSZ_* enum prefixes, sysz_* type names, and detail->sysz have been renamed to SYSTEMZ_*, systemz_*, and detail->systemz respectively.
quote: Simple renaming from `SYSZ` to `SYSTEMZ`

from_fact: sysz_* (e.g. cs_arch_sysz, sysz_ins_*, sysz_reg_*, sysz_op_*, sysz_cc_*) and sysz_* types (sysz_reg, sysz_cc, cs_sysz, sysz_op_type, sysz_op_mem, sysz_op) and detail->sysz

subject: cstool command-line syntax for enabling syntax options
old: cstool required modifying C code and recompiling to enable syntax features
new: cstool now accepts +suffix options appended to the arch argument (e.g., x32+att, ppc32+percentage)
truth: Stop modifying C code and recompiling cstool to enable syntax options; instead append +suffix to the arch argument (e.g., x32+att).
quote: cstool has been refactored to simplify its usage; before you needed to add extra options in the C code to enable features and recompile, but now you can easily decode instructions with different syntaxes or options, by appending after the arch one of the followings values:

from_fact: cstool required modifying c code and recompiling to enable syntax features
cstool now accepts +suffix options appended to the arch argument (e.g., x32+att, ppc32+percentage)
